title: Add universal command palette for instant manuscript navigation and actions
description:
## Problem
As manuscripts grow, writers lose momentum when they must hunt through sidebar hierarchies to find scenes, chapters, notes, or common actions. Navigation friction breaks drafting flow and increases context-switch cost.

## Target user
Writers actively drafting or revising medium-to-long manuscripts (20+ scenes) who need fast keyboard-first movement.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce median time-to-open-target-scene during revision sessions
- Increase weekly usage of keyboard navigation workflows
- Improve session depth (more words/edits per session after feature adoption)

## Scope
- Add a command palette opened by keyboard shortcut (e.g., Cmd/Ctrl+K)
- Support fuzzy search across stories, chapters, scenes, and worldbuilding entities
- Include quick actions: create scene, rename scene, jump to timeline/corkboard, open story settings
- Show recent items and last-opened scenes for one-step resume
- Preserve current view mode when possible when jumping between scenes

## Non-goals
- AI-generated commands or natural-language intent parsing
- Replacing existing sidebar or navigator UI
- Full-text semantic search over manuscript body in v1

## Acceptance criteria
- User can open/close palette entirely with keyboard
- Search results include at least stories, chapters, scenes, and worldbuilding entities
- Selecting a result navigates to the correct destination in one action
- Quick actions perform successfully and provide immediate feedback
- Palette remains responsive with 200+ scenes in local test data

## Priority rationale
Now that navigator, corkboard, timeline, and worldbuilding surfaces are expanding, retrieval speed becomes a core UX bottleneck. This feature directly supports Living Writer-style clarity while giving a differentiated speed advantage for power users.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Link worldbuilding entities inline in the editor with mention chips and quick peek
description:
## Problem
Writers track characters, places, and lore in separate panels, but references inside prose are not structurally linked. This creates continuity drift and forces manual cross-checking during revision.

## Target user
Fiction writers managing recurring characters/locations/rules who need context while drafting without leaving the editor.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase usage of linked worldbuilding entities per manuscript
- Reduce continuity-related revisions tied to character/location inconsistency
- Increase frequency of worldbuilding panel interactions during drafting sessions

## Scope
- Add inline mention trigger in editor (e.g., @) to insert linked entity chips for character/location/rule items
- Store and render mentions as durable links to worldbuilding records
- Add hover/click quick peek card showing key entity details without full panel switch
- Provide mention management in scene metadata or inspector (list all mentions in current scene)
- Keep plain-text export fallback by converting mention chips to readable text labels

## Non-goals
- Automatic NER/entity detection from raw prose
- Complex bi-directional graph analytics in v1
- Multi-user mention resolution workflows

## Acceptance criteria
- User can insert entity mentions from the editor via keyboard and mouse
- Mention chips render consistently after reload and scene switches
- Clicking/hovering a mention opens a lightweight entity peek with core fields
- Removing an entity from worldbuilding does not corrupt manuscript text
- Export output remains readable when mentions are present

## Priority rationale
Campfire demonstrates demand for strong world context, but often in separate modules. Inline linking keeps the writer in flow and can become a differentiation point: context-rich drafting without UI sprawl.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Ship draft milestone planner with chapter-level progress and finish-date forecast
description:
## Problem
Many writers start projects but fail to finish because progress is not translated into a concrete manuscript completion path. Existing writing views show content, not a clear trajectory to “The End.”

## Target user
Writers in active first-draft phase who need lightweight accountability and visible momentum.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active stories reaching “draft complete” status
- Improve week-over-week writing consistency for users who enable milestones
- Reduce abandonment rate after first 2 weeks of use

## Scope
- Add optional draft milestone setup: target draft length, target completion date, weekly writing cadence
- Compute chapter-level progress against draft plan and show remaining effort
- Surface a forecast indicator (on-track / at-risk) based on recent writing velocity
- Add dashboard entry in story workspace with actionable next milestone
- Allow users to pause, edit, or disable milestones without affecting manuscript data

## Non-goals
- Public accountability/community leaderboards
- Punitive streak mechanics or gamification-heavy UX
- AI-generated writing content to meet goals

## Acceptance criteria
- User can configure a milestone plan in under 2 minutes
- System shows current progress, next milestone, and forecast state for active story
- Forecast updates after writing sessions without manual refresh
- User can edit or disable milestone plan at any time
- Milestone data persists per story across reloads

## Priority rationale
Onboarding gets users started, but completion mechanics keep them through the full manuscript lifecycle. Shipping this now strengthens retention and aligns with user need to finish stories, not just organize them.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
